title: The Patch Bay
shortDescription: Where you stop just printing things and start telling the machine what to decide.
longDescription: Named for the wall of telephone-style jacks and cables still bolted to the workbench here — Meridian's researchers borrowed the lab's phone equipment to route signals between early experiments long before anyone called it 'programming.' Restoring the Patch Bay means learning to route your own logic: variables to hold values, operators to combine them, branches and loops to decide and repeat, and functions to wire it all into reusable pieces.
